text: The Southern District of Indiana sanctioned attorney Tae Sture $2,000 under Rule 11 after his firm's paralegal used the Fastcase legal research tool, which generated hallucinated citations that were filed without verification. The court characterized the citations as "hallucinations generated by an artificial intelligence research tool" and specifically named Fastcase. The district judge reduced the Magistrate's recommended $7,500 sanction to $2,000.
canonical_source_conflicted: Genuine federal employment and civil-rights caselaw; Fed. R. Civ. P. 11
mitigation_gap: Paralegal used Fastcase AI without verification; supervising attorney failed to review citations before filing.
reliance_or_harm: $2,000 Rule 11 sanction (reduced from $7,500 recommended); AI-hallucinated citations submitted in federal employment litigation.
